sections.label: Section 1374
sections.original: οἷα δʼ εἰσῆλθέν μʼ , ἄκουσον , μῆτερ , ἐννοουμένην ·
sections.translation: Listen , mother ; hear what thoughts have passed across my mind .

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: κατθανεῖν μέν μοι δέδοκται · τοῦτο δʼ αὐτὸ βούλομαι
sections.translation: I am resolved to die ; and this I want to do with honor , dismissing from me what is mean . Towards this now , mother turn your thoughts , and with me weigh how well I speak ; to me the whole of mighty Hellas looks ; on me the passage over the sea depends ; on me the sack of Troy ;

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: τάς τε μελλούσας γυναῖκας , ἤν τι δρῶσι βάρβαροι ,
sections.translation: and in my power it lies to check henceforth barbarian raids on happy Hellas , if ever in the days to come they seek to seize her women , when once they have atoned by death [*] for the violation of Helen ’ s marriage by Paris . All this deliverance will my death insure , and my fame for setting Hellas free will be a happy one .

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: καὶ γὰρ οὐδέ τοί τι λίαν ἐμὲ φιλοψυχεῖν χρεών ·
sections.translation: Besides , I have no right at all to cling too fondly to my life ; for you did not bear me for myself alone , but as a public blessing to all Hellas . What ! shall countless warriors , armed with shields , those myriads sitting at the oar , find courage to attack the foe and die for Hellas , because their fatherland is wronged ,

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: ἡ δʼ ἐμὴ ψυχὴ μίʼ οὖσα πάντα κωλύσει τάδε ;
sections.translation: and my one life prevent all this ? What [*] kind of justice is that ? could I find a word in answer ? Now let us turn to that other point . It is not right that this man should enter into battle with all Argos or be slain for a woman ’ s sake . Better a single man should see the light than ten thousand women .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: εἰ βεβούληται δὲ σῶμα τοὐμὸν Ἄρτεμις λαβεῖν ,
sections.translation: If Artemis has decided to take my body , am I , a mortal , to thwart the goddess ? no , that is impossible . I give my body to Hellas ; sacrifice it and make an utter end of Troy . This is my enduring monument ; marriage , motherhood , and fame — all these is it to me .

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: βαρβάρων δʼ Ἕλληνας ἄρχειν εἰκός , ἀλλʼ οὐ βαρβάρους ,
sections.translation: And it is right , mother , that Hellenes should rule barbarians , but not barbarians Hellenes , those being slaves , while these are free .

sections.label: Section 1402
sections.original: τὸ μὲν σόν , ὦ νεᾶνι , γενναίως ἔχει ·
sections.translation: You play a noble part , maiden ; but the whims of Fate and the goddess are diseased .

sections.label: Section 1403
sections.original: τὸ τῆς τύχης δὲ καὶ τὸ τῆς θεοῦ νοσεῖ .
sections.translation: Daughter of Agamemnon ! some god was bent

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: ἔμελλε θήσειν , εἰ τύχοιμι σῶν γάμων .
sections.translation: on blessing me , if I could have won you for my wife . In you I consider Hellas happy , and you in Hellas ; for this that you have said is good and worthy of your fatherland ; since you , abandonIng a strife with heavenly powers , which are too strong for you , have fairly weighed advantages and needs . [*]

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: μᾶλλον δὲ λέκτρων σῶν πόθος μʼ ἐσέρχεται
sections.translation: But now that I have looked into your noble nature , I feel still more a fond desire to win you for my bride . Look to it ; for I want to serve you and receive you in my halls ; and , Thetis be my witness , how I grieve to think I shall not save your life by doing battle with the Danaids .

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: ἐλθών . ἄθρησον · ὁ θάνατος δεινὸν κακόν .
sections.translation: Reflect , I say ; a dreadful ill is death .

sections.label: Section 1416
sections.original: λέγω τάδʼ οὐδὲν οὐδένʼ εὐλαβουμένη .
sections.translation: This I say , without regard to anyone . [*] Enough that the daughter of Tyndareus is causing wars and bloodshed by her beauty ; then be not slain yourself , stranger , nor seek to slay another on my account ;

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: ἔα δὲ σῷσαί μʼ Ἑλλάδʼ , ἢν δυνώμεθα .
sections.translation: but let me , if I can , save Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1421
sections.original: ὦ λῆμʼ ἄριστον , οὐκ ἔχω πρὸς τοῦτʼ ἔτι
sections.translation: Heroic spirit ! I can say no more to this , since you are so minded ; for yours is a noble resolve ; why should not one speak the truth ? Yet I will speak , for you will perhaps change your mind ;

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: ὡς οὖν ἂν εἰδῇς τἀπʼ ἐμοῦ λελεγμένα ,
sections.translation: that you may know then what my offer is , I will go and place these arms of mine near the altar , resolved not to permit your death but to prevent ; for brave as you are at sight of the knife held at your throat , you will soon avail yourself of what I said .

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐάσω σʼ ἀφροσύνῃ τῇ σῇ θανεῖν ·
sections.translation: So I will not let you perish through any thoughtlessness of yours , but will go to the goddess with these arms and await your arrival there . [*] Exit Achilles .

sections.label: Section 1433
sections.original: μῆτερ , τί σιγῇ δακρύοις τέγγεις κόρας ;
sections.translation: Mother , why so silent , your eyes wet with tears ?

sections.label: Section 1434
sections.original: ἔχω τάλαινα πρόφασιν ὥστʼ ἀλγεῖν φρένα .
sections.translation: I have reason , woe is me ! to be sad at heart .

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: παῦσαι · ʼμὲ μὴ κάκιζε · τάδε δέ μοι πιθοῦ .
sections.translation: Stop ; do not make me a coward ; here in one thing obey me .

sections.label: Section 1436
sections.original: λέγʼ · ὡς παρʼ ἡμῶν οὐδὲν ἀδικήσῃ , τέκνον .
sections.translation: Tell me , my child , for at my hands you shall never suffer injury .

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.original: μήτʼ οὖν γε τὸν σὸν πλόκαμον ἐκτέμῃς τριχός ,
sections.translation: Cut not off the tresses of your hair for me , nor clothe yourself in sable garb . [*]

sections.label: Section 1439
sections.original: τί δὴ τόδʼ εἶπας , τέκνον ; ἀπολέσασά σε ;
sections.translation: Why , my child , what is it you have said ? When I have lost you ? [*]

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: οὐ σύ γε · σέσῳσμαι , κατʼ ἐμὲ δʼ εὐκλεὴς ἔσῃ .
sections.translation: You wll not lose me ; I am saved and you renowned , as far as I can make you .

sections.label: Section 1441
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; οὐ πενθεῖν με σὴν ψυχὴν χρεών ;
sections.translation: How so ? Must I not mourn your death ?

sections.label: Section 1442
sections.original: ἥκιστʼ , ἐπεί μοι τύμβος οὐ χωσθήσεται .
sections.translation: By no means , for I shall have no tomb heaped over me .

sections.label: Section 1443
sections.original: τί δαί ; τὸ θνῄσκειν , οὐ τάφος , νομίζεται .
sections.translation: What then ? It is death , not the tomb , that is rightly mourned .

sections.label: Section 1444
sections.original: βωμὸς θεᾶς μοι μνῆμα τῆς Διὸς κόρης .
sections.translation: The altar of the goddess , Zeus ’ s daughter , will be my tomb .

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ τέκνον , σοὶ πείσομαι · λέγεις γὰρ εὖ .
sections.translation: Well , my child , I will let you persuade me , for you speak well .

sections.label: Section 1446
sections.original: ὡς εὐτυχοῦσά γʼ Ἑλλάδος τʼ εὐεργέτις .
sections.translation: Yes , as one who prospers and does Hellas service .

sections.label: Section 1447
sections.original: τί δὴ κασιγνήταισιν ἀγγελῶ σέθεν ;
sections.translation: What message shall I carry to your sisters ?

sections.label: Section 1448
sections.original: μηδʼ ἀμφὶ κείναις μέλανας ἐξάψῃ πέπλους .
sections.translation: Do not put mourning raiment on them either .

sections.label: Section 1449
sections.original: εἴπω δὲ παρὰ σοῦ φίλον ἔπος τι παρθένοις ;
sections.translation: But is there no fond message I can give the maidens from you ?

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: χαίρειν γε . Ὀρέστην τʼ ἔκτρεφʼ ἄνδρα τόνδε μοι .
sections.translation: Yes , my farewell words ; and promise me to rear Orestes to manhood .

sections.label: Section 1451
sections.original: προσέλκυσαί νιν ὕστατον θεωμένη .
sections.translation: Press him to your bosom ; it is your last look .

sections.label: Section 1452
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , ἐπεκούρησας ὅσον εἶχες φίλοις .
sections.translation: O you that are most dear to me ! you have helped your friends as you had means .

sections.label: Section 1453
sections.original: ἔσθʼ ὅ τι κατʼ Ἄργος δρῶσά σοι χάριν φέρω ;
sections.translation: Is there anything I can do in Argos to please you ?

sections.label: Section 1454
sections.original: πατέρα τὸν ἀμὸν μὴ στύγει , πόσιν γε σόν .
sections.translation: Yes , do not hate my father , your own husband .

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: δεινοὺς ἀγῶνας διὰ σὲ δεῖ κεῖνον δραμεῖν .
sections.translation: Fearful are the trials through which he has to go because of you .

sections.label: Section 1456
sections.original: ἄκων μʼ ὑπὲρ γῆς Ἑλλάδος διώλεσεν .
sections.translation: It was against his will he ruined me for the sake of Hellas .

sections.label: Section 1457
sections.original: δόλῳ δʼ , ἀγεννῶς Ἀτρέως τʼ οὐκ ἀξίως .
sections.translation: Ah ! but he employed base treachery , unworthy of Atreus .

sections.label: Section 1458
sections.original: τίς μʼ εἶσιν ἄξων πρὶν σπαράσσεσθαι κόμης ;
sections.translation: Who will escort me from here , before my hair is torn ?

sections.label: Section 1459
sections.original: ἐγώ , μετά γε σοῦ —
sections.translation: I will go with you —

sections.label: Section 1459b
sections.original: μὴ σύ γʼ · οὐ καλῶς λέγεις .
sections.translation: No , not you ; that is not well saidl .

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: πέπλων ἐχομένη σῶν .
sections.translation: Clinging to your robes .

sections.label: Section 1460b
sections.original: ἐμοί , μῆτερ , πιθοῦ ·
sections.translation: Be persuaded by me , mother , stay here ; for this is the better way both for me and you ; but let one of these attendants of my father conduct me to the meadow of Artemis , where I shall be sacrificed .

sections.label: Section 1464
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , οἴχῃ ;
sections.translation: Are you gone from me , my child ?

sections.label: Section 1464b
sections.original: καὶ πάλιν γʼ οὐ μὴ μόλω .
sections.translation: Yes , and with no chance of ever returning .

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: λιποῦσα μητέρα ;
sections.translation: Leaving your mother ?

sections.label: Section 1465b
sections.original: ὡς ὁρᾷς γʼ , οὐκ ἀξίως .
sections.translation: Yes , as you see , undeservedly .

sections.label: Section 1466
sections.original: σχές , μή με προλίπῃς .
sections.translation: Hold ! do not leave me !

sections.label: Section 1466b
sections.original: οὐκ ἐῶ στάζειν δάκρυ .
sections.translation: I cannot let you shed a tear . To the Chorus . May it be yours , maidens , to hymn in joyous strains Artemis , the child of Zeus , for my hard lot ; and let the order for a solemn hush go forth to the Danaids .

sections.label: Section 1470
sections.original: κανᾶ δʼ ἐναρχέσθω τις , αἰθέσθω δὲ πῦρ
sections.translation: Begin the sacrifice with the baskets , let the fire blaze for the purifying meal of sprinkling , and my father pace from left to right about the altar ; for I come to bestow on Hellas safety crowned with victory .

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: ἄγετέ με τὰν Ἰλίου
sections.translation: Lead me away , the destroyer of Ilium ’ s town and the Phrygians ; give me wreaths to cast about me ; bring them here ; here are my tresses to crown ; bring lustral water too .

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: ἑλίσσετʼ ἀμφὶ ναὸν
sections.translation: Dance to Artemis , queen Artemis the blest , around her shrine and altar ; for by the blood of my sacrifice I will blot out the oracle ,

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: αἵμασι θύμασί τε
sections.translation: if it must be .

sections.label: Section 1487
sections.original: ὦ πότνια πότνια μᾶτερ , οὐ δάκρυά γέ σοι
sections.translation: O mother , lady revered ! I will , not give you my tears ;

sections.label: Section 1490
sections.original: παρʼ ἱεροῖς γὰρ οὐ πρέπει .
sections.translation: for at the holy rites it is not fitting . [*]

sections.label: Section 1491
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰὼ νεάνιδες ,
sections.translation: Sing with me , maidens , sing the praises of Artemis , whose temple faces Chalcis ,

sections.label: Section 1495
sections.original: ἵνα τε δόρατα μέμονε δάϊʼ
sections.translation: where angry spearmen madly chafe , here in the narrow havens of Aulis , because of me .
